free_text: New results on helium and tritium gas production from
data tables for H-3 and He-4 yields are given
C.Wagemans, Proc. Seminar on fission,"Pont d'Oye IV",
for Pu239, Pu241, Cm247(n-th,f) and Pu238, Pu240,
Proc. of 5th Int.Conf. on Dynamical Aspects of Nucl.
World Scientific, p.319 - Cm245(n-th,f) TER/BIN ratio
Proc. 3d Int. Conf. on Fission and Properties of
Neutron-Rich Nuclei, Sanibel Island, USA, Nov.2002,
Proc. Seminar on Fission, "Pont d'Oye V", Habay-la-
2004, p.151 - Cm246(0,f) TER/BIN ratio yield data
alpha-energy spectra. Ternary alpha emission yield
a vacuum chamber with a sample in its centre and
a silicon surface barrier telescope detector mounted
Telescope consisted of a 15.4-mum thick delta-E
detector, area 150 mm**2, alpha energy resolution 50
keV and a 500-mum thick E-detector, area 450 mm-sq,
alpha energy resolution 19 keV. The energy calibration
of the detector was done using the well-known energies
Nd-143(n,alpha)Ce-140 reactions and of the U-234,
U-235 and Cf-252 radio-active decay alphas
The measuring system was first tested with the
spontaneous fission Cf-252, using a source with an
activity of 70 KbQ. A 30-mum thick Al-foil was placed
in between the source and the telescope in order to
The uncertainty is reported by authors in
